other_observers: Eric DeFonso, Leah Colton; I am original finder and identifier
specific_location: Pond near historic Strauss Cabin site, just west of City of Fort Collins Archery Park, along Cache La Poudre River
description: Typical adult white-headed gull in winter, that is white underparts and head with gray-brown smudging on nape and crown; gray mantle and wings, with black and white wing tips, white tail. Superficially similar to accompanying adult Ring-billed Gulls, but overall about 10% smaller, with rounder head, apparently darker eye, smaller bill with only a dark smudge rather than a well-defined ring; thinner legs; darker gray wings and mantle; large white scapular crescent; larger white mirror on tenth primary.Behaviors: Preening and loafing on rocky sandbar in a small lake derived from an old gravel pit.Call: not heardPlumage: adult basic
photographer: Photographer: Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar
